talk_metadata.talk_title: Serial Plasma Comprehensive Genomic Profiling Detects Therapy Resistance and Guides Management of Metastatic Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "patients who received a genotype-targeted therapy based on an actionable alteration identified by PCGP had significantly better overall survival compared to those who did not."
Speaker said: "we were able to capture these structural alterations by liquid biopsy, but there were some instances of discordance."

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Low tumor burden or intrathoracic disease reduces assay sensitivity for MET amplification

key_data_gaps: No overall survival data stratified by specific resistance mechanisms
Limited concordance analysis between tissue and plasma MET amplification

crispro_opportunity.description: CrisPRO could model MET amplification–driven resistance pathways and suggest combination strategies to overcome detection gaps.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker noted MET amplification was only captured in ~10% of progression samples and missed in some cases due to technical challenges.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Use computational pathway analysis to predict co‑occurring resistance alterations and prioritize agents that target MET plus parallel pathways.

cited_competitors.name: Guardant 360
cited_competitors.context: used as the liquid biopsy assay platform

unresolved_questions: What is the optimal assay sensitivity needed to reliably detect MET amplification in low‑burden disease?

talk_metadata.institution_or_pharma: University of Pittsburgh Hillman Cancer Center
talk_metadata.talk_title: Feasibility of Plasma ctDNA for Detecting Brain Metastasis Specific Alterations in Lung Cancer
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "MET amplification was significantly more common in the brain metastases at a rate of 16.4% compared to 3.7% in primary lung tumors."
Speaker said: "sensitivity still remains a major limitation"

vulnerability_identified.failing_compound_or_target: Standard ctDNA assays for brain metastasis MET amplification
vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Blood‑brain barrier limits ctDNA shedding from intracranial lesions

trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: Detection of MET amplification in brain metastases via plasma ctDNA
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: No requirement for timing of blood draw relative to radiotherapy or surgery, leading to variable ctDNA shedding
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: Concurrent CSF ctDNA or imaging‑guided sampling
trial_dilution_risk.static_vs_dynamic_failure: Lack of serial post‑radiation sampling to capture peak shedding

key_data_gaps: Small number of paired tissue‑plasma samples
No systematic evaluation of assay sensitivity thresholds

crispro_opportunity.description: CrisPRO can integrate ctDNA VAF dynamics with radiotherapy timing to define optimal sampling windows for brain metastasis detection.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker highlighted increased ctDNA detection after postoperative radiation in a case study.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Model temporal shedding kinetics to recommend post‑radiation blood draw intervals.

cited_competitors.name: Predicinn CARE 200 gene NGS assay
cited_competitors.context: used for deep sequencing of plasma and tissue samples

unresolved_questions: What is the minimal tumor fraction required for reliable MET amplification detection in brain metastasis ctDNA?

talk_metadata.talk_title: AXL mRNA Overexpression in CTCs as a New Potential Liquid Biopsy Biomarker in Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "AXL overexpression was detected in almost 13% of patients at baseline before treatment, at 16% after the first cycle of therapy..."
Speaker said: "our results indicate that longitudinal monitoring ... could be a precious tool for the assessment of osimertinib efficacy and early therapeutic intervention with AXL inhibitors."

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker noted multiple AXL‑targeting drugs are in clinical trials, suggesting a crowded IP space.
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Reliance on bulk CTC RNA measurement may miss low‑frequency AXL‑positive clones.

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that bulk CTC RNA detects AXL overexpression in ~13% of patients, then concluded that AXL‑positive CTCs could guide therapy despite acknowledging limited sensitivity of bulk approach.

key_data_gaps: No concordance data between CTC AXL expression and tissue AXL status
Therapeutic threshold for AXL RNA levels not defined

crispro_opportunity.description: CrisPRO could define quantitative AXL expression cutoffs predictive of response to AXL inhibitors.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker highlighted variable AXL overexpression across treatment cycles without a defined therapeutic threshold.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Apply machine‑learning on longitudinal CTC RNA data to derive response‑linked expression thresholds.

cited_competitors.name: Parsortix system
cited_competitors.context: size‑based CTC enrichment platform used in the study

unresolved_questions: What AXL RNA expression level constitutes a clinically actionable target?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Circulating Tumor Cell‑Informed Pipeline for Rapid Personalization of Synergistic Cytokine‑Armed CAR‑NK Cell Therapy in Pancreatic Ductal Adenocarcinoma
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "87% of patients carry CTCs with at least one of our CAR‑NK cell targets."
Speaker said: "IL‑12 and IL‑15 armed CAR‑NK cells can actually exert a very long‑term killing efficiencies."

trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: CTC‑guided selection of mesothelin or MUC4 for CAR‑NK therapy
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: No pre‑screening for target expression in metastatic lesions; reliance on CTCs alone may miss intratumoral heterogeneity
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: Serial tissue biopsies to confirm target persistence
trial_dilution_risk.static_vs_dynamic_failure: Lack of longitudinal CTC monitoring after CAR‑NK infusion

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Proprietary design disclosed in talk; competitors could replicate size‑based enrichment with alternative platforms.
competitive_moat_weakness.scale_or_manufacturing_bottleneck: Device scaling for clinical‑grade GMP production not addressed
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Target selection limited to two antigens; may not cover full PDAC heterogeneity.

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker demonstrated high correlation between CTC and primary tumor target expression, yet admitted lack of metastatic tissue validation, creating a gap between assumed representativeness and actual evidence.

key_data_gaps: No in‑human efficacy or safety data for cytokine‑armed CAR‑NK cells
Limited patient numbers with matched CTC‑tumor validation

crispro_opportunity.description: CrisPRO could predict emergence of antigen‑loss variants from CTC dynamics to guide sequential CAR‑NK target switching.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker noted exclusive MUC4 or mesothelin CTC populations and potential target loss over time.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Model clonal evolution using longitudinal CTC proteomics to anticipate escape mechanisms.

unresolved_questions: How stable are mesothelin/MUC4 expressions on CTCs after prior lines of therapy?

assets_to_track: CAR‑NK platforms targeting mesothelin or MUC4

talk_metadata.talk_title: ctDNA Clearance Dynamics in MSI‑high Colorectal Cancer Treated with Immune Checkpoint Inhibitors
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "77% achieved ctDNA clearance at least one time point after treatment initiation with a median time to clearance of 96 days."
Speaker said: "Patients who had a negative ctDNA result at this time point had significantly superior overall survival..."

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: No molecular correlates (e.g., TCR repertoire) integrated to explain persistent ctDNA positivity
vulnerability_identified.unexplained_resistance_quote: Speaker noted persistent ctDNA positivity was associated with markedly inferior overall survival but did not explain underlying biology.

key_data_gaps: Lack of uniform ctDNA sampling timepoints
No integration of TCR or immune profiling

crispro_opportunity.description: CrisPRO could integrate ctDNA dynamics with tumor‑infiltrating lymphocyte signatures to refine early response prediction.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker highlighted early ctDNA negativity as a strong prognostic marker but lacked immune correlates.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Combine ctDNA VAF trajectories with computational immune repertoire modeling.

cited_competitors.name: Signatera (Natera)
cited_competitors.context: tumor‑informed ctDNA assay used for monitoring

unresolved_questions: Does ctDNA clearance kinetics differ between pembrolizumab monotherapy and ipilimumab/nivolumab combination?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Genomic Signatures in Red Blood Cell DNA‑Enabled Early Non‑Invasive Detection of Colorectal Neoplasia
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "Our model achieved an overall AUC of 95% for detecting advanced colorectal neoplasia with 85% sensitivity for advanced adenomas and 95% sensitivity for colorectal cancer."
Speaker said: "Sensitivity for advanced adenomas was over 60% in external validation, higher than stool‑based tests."

competitive_moat_weakness.vulnerable_ip_or_chemistry: RBC‑DNA extraction and low‑coverage sequencing workflow
competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Methodology described in detail; could be replicated by competitors with similar sequencing platforms.
competitive_moat_weakness.scale_or_manufacturing_bottleneck: Requires specialized RBC isolation and high‑throughput sequencing infrastructure.
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Performance drops for advanced adenomas (sensitivity ~60%) compared to cancer.

key_data_gaps: Biological origin of RBC‑DNA signatures not fully elucidated
Limited data on performance in non‑colorectal cancers

crispro_opportunity.description: CrisPRO could apply the RBC‑DNA signature pipeline to other GI cancers by training on pan‑cancer datasets.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker mentioned pan‑cancer RBC‑DNA signals in prior work.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Leverage existing methylation‑based classifiers to extend to gastric and pancreatic neoplasia.

unresolved_questions: Do inflammatory or non‑malignant conditions affect RBC‑DNA signatures?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Methylation‑Based Molecular Tumor Typing Classifier for Tissue Samples from Cancers of Unknown Primary
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "On 4,000 tissue samples, we observed 97% evaluable rate and 91% accuracy with the primary prediction."
Speaker said: "For CUP cases, we achieved 88% accuracy on primary prediction and 94% when including secondary prediction."

competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Relies on proprietary DMR panel; competitors could develop alternative methylation panels.
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Requires ≥5% tumor fraction; low‑purity samples may still be unevaluable.

key_data_gaps: Limited prospective validation in real‑world CUP workflow
Unclear performance on early‑stage or low‑purity specimens below 5% tumor fraction

crispro_opportunity.description: CrisPRO can augment methylation‑based typing with integrated mutation and expression data to improve confidence for low‑purity CUP samples.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker noted confidence levels correlate with accuracy, suggesting room for multimodal improvement.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: Combine methylation DMR scores with tumor‑informed mutation signatures to boost predictive power.

unresolved_questions: How does the classifier perform on CUP cases with tumor fraction <5%?
